groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

content: Good for people who are especially keen on $21955$

Quite expensive
The $21956$ is a good place for a drink
In Bow Street next to the cinema

Staff are very friendly
Need to pay £50 deposit
A limited selection of $21958$ food on the menu

A famous chef
All the $21961$ are very good
Only uses $21962$ ingredients

Set lunch costs: £$21963$ per person
Portions probably of $21964$ size

questions.analysis: 同学你好，我们来看这道题。题目问的是The Junction餐厅特别适合热衷于什么的人。这里的“especially keen on”（特别热衷于）是定位词。在听力原文中，男士提到“The food's amazing. If you like fish it's probably the best restaurant in town for that.”，这里的“like”就是“especially keen on”的同义替换，是重要的信号词。这句话明确指出如果你喜欢“fish”（鱼），这家餐厅是城里最好的，所以答案自然就是“fish”。虽然前面提到了“food”很棒，后面也提到了“dishes”，但“food”太宽泛，“dishes”指的是菜式，而题目问的是“热衷于”的具体对象，“fish”才是那个被特别推荐的、适合热衷者的具体品类。所以这道题考察的就是通过同义替换“like”来定位，并捕捉到具体信息“fish”。

题目问的是“The Junction”餐厅里“适合喝酒的好地方”是什么。首先，我们通过题干中的定位词“drink”去听力中找相关信息。当听到男士说“have a drink”（这是“drink”的同义表达，也就是信号词）时，就要注意前后内容。前面提到“go up on the roof”，意思是“上到屋顶”，结合题干“a good place for a drink”，显然这里的“roof”（屋顶）就是那个适合喝酒的地方，所以答案是“roof”。

这道题问的是Paloma餐厅的推荐原因中“[___] food”的内容。首先，我们通过定位词“Paloma”找到关于这家餐厅的对话部分。接着，题干中的“sharing”提示我们要关注与“分享”相关的描述，听力中男士提到“small dishes to share”，这里的“share”就是“sharing”的同义替换，说明答案就在附近。女士询问“is all the food they serve Spanish, then?”，男士用“Yeah”确认，直接点明了食物种类是“Spanish”。所以，“Spanish”是正确答案，它准确回答了“推荐原因中的食物种类”这一问题，且与后面“good for sharing”的描述逻辑一致。

同学你看，这道题问的是Paloma餐厅菜单上“a limited selection of ... food”（选择有限的某种食物）。首先，我们在题目中找到定位词“limited selection”，它在听力原文中被男士用“quite limited”这个信号词替换了。当听到男士说“the selection of those would be quite limited”时，就要注意“those”指代的是什么。往前听，女士提到她的朋友“stopped eating meat”（不吃肉了），所以她要查看菜单上的“vegetarian dishes”（素食菜肴）。因此，男士说的“selection of those”指的就是“vegetarian dishes”。题目要求填入修饰“food”的词，所以“vegetarian”（素食的）就是正确答案，它准确描述了那种选择有限的食物类型。

表格按餐厅顺序依次介绍，前两家餐厅（The Junction和Paloma）讨论结束后，男士用“I’ve just thought of another idea”引出第三家餐厅，这是明确的定位信号，说明接下来要介绍的就是表格中的第三家餐厅。随后男士直接提到餐厅名“Have you been to The Audley?”，女士追问拼写“How’s it spelt?”，男士逐字母拼出“A-U-D-L-E-Y”，进一步确认了餐厅名称的正确性。因此，表格中第三家餐厅的名字应填“Audley”。

同学你好，我们来看第6题。题目问的是“The Audley”餐厅的位置，具体是“At the top of a 【6】”。首先，我们用“The Audley”这个餐厅名作为定位词，当听到对话中提到“The Audley”时，就要集中注意力听它的位置描述。接着，题目中的“At the top of”是关键的定位信息，在听力原文中，男士说“It’s in that hotel near Baxter Bridge – on the top floor.”，这里的“on the top floor”（在顶层）就是“At the top of”的同义替换，是重要的信号词。这句话明确指出餐厅在“hotel”（酒店）的顶层，所以“At the top of a”后面应该填的就是“hotel”。

同学你好，我们来看这道题。题目问的是The Audley餐厅的推荐理由之一“All the ______ are very good”。首先，我们可以根据表格中上一行的“famous chef”（著名厨师）作为定位词。在听力原文中，当提到这家餐厅的厨师Angela Frayn时，她曾是电视烹饪节目的评委还写过烹饪书，这些信息都对应了“famous chef”这个定位词，帮助我们锁定了相关的段落。紧接着，男士说“Anyway, it’s had excellent reviews from all the newspapers.”，这里的“excellent”（极好的）和题目中的“very good”是同义替换。所以，“excellent”所修饰的“reviews”（评价）就是我们要填的答案，因为这句话明确表示这家餐厅的所有评价都非常好，正好符合题目“All the ______ are very good”的要求。注意不要错填成“newspapers”，因为“newspapers”是评价的来源，而不是被描述为“very good”的对象。

这道题问的是The Audley餐厅只使用什么食材。我们首先看题目中的定位词“ingredients”（食材）。在听力原文中，当讨论到The Audley餐厅的厨师Angela Frayn时，女士提到“She only likes cooking with local products”，这里的“products”就是“ingredients”的同义替换词。男士接着肯定并补充说明“Everything at the restaurant has to be sourced within a short distance...”，“sourced”（采购）也与使用食材相关。“local products”明确指出了是“本地的产品”，也就是“本地的食材”，所以“ingredients”前面应该填“local”。男士后面说的“within a short distance”（短距离内）和“nothing flown in from abroad”（不从国外空运）都是对“local”（本地的）的进一步解释，因此答案是“local”。
这道题的定位词是“Set lunch”，在听力原文中，当男士提到“set lunch”时，就提示我们要关注套餐午餐的价格了。答案句明确说“£30 a head”，这里的“a head”和题干中的“per person”意思相同，都是“每人”的意思，所以套餐午餐的价格就是每人30英镑，因此第9题的答案是30。

题目问的是The Audley餐厅“Portions probably of 【10】 size”，定位词是“Portions”。在听力中，女生提到“With tiny portions?”（份量是不是很小？），直接出现了定位词“portions”，这是答案即将出现的信号。男生回答“No, the reviews I’ve read didn’t mention that. I imagine they’d be average.”，首先用“No”否定了“tiny”（小份量），然后明确指出“they’d be average”（份量是中等的）。因此，“average”就是描述份量大小的正确答案。

transcript.en: PART 1

WOMAN: I've been meaning to ask you for some advice about restaurants – I need to book somewhere to celebrate my sister's 30th birthday and I liked the sound of that place you went to for your mum's 50th.
transcript.cn: 女人：我一直想问你一些关于餐厅的建议 —— 我需要预订一个地方庆祝我妹妹的 30 岁生日，我喜欢你为你妈妈庆祝 50 岁生日时去的那家餐厅的声音。

transcript.en: MAN: The Junction? Yeah, I'd definitely recommend that for a special occasion.
transcript.cn: 男人：枢纽站？是的，如果是特殊场合，我肯定会推荐那里。

transcript.en: We had a great time there. Everyone really enjoyed it.
transcript.cn: 我们在那里度过了一段美好的时光。每个人都真的很享受。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Where is it again? I can't remember.
transcript.cn: 女人：在哪儿来着？我记不清了。

transcript.en: MAN: It's on Greyson Street, only about a two minute walk from the station.
transcript.cn: 男人：在格雷森街，从车站走过去大约两分钟。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Oh, that's good. I'd prefer not to have to drive anywhere.
transcript.cn: 女人：哦，那太好了。我可不想开车去任何地方。

transcript.en: But I don't want to have to walk too far either.
transcript.cn: 但我也不想走太远。

transcript.en: MAN: Yes, the location's perfect but that's not necessarily why I'd recommend it.
transcript.cn: 男人：是的，地理位置很完美，但这不一定是我推荐它的原因。

transcript.en: The food's amazing. If you like fish it's probably the best restaurant in town for that.
transcript.cn: 食物棒极了。如果你喜欢吃鱼，这里可能是城里最好的餐厅。

transcript.en: It's always really fresh and there are lots of interesting dishes to choose from.
transcript.cn: 它总是非常新鲜，而且有很多有趣的菜可以选择。

transcript.en: But all the food is good there.
transcript.cn: 但那里的食物都很好。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Is it really expensive?
transcript.cn: 女人：真的很贵吗？

transcript.en: MAN: It's certainly not cheap but for a special occasion I think it's fine.
transcript.cn: 男人：当然不便宜，但对于特殊场合来说，我觉得还不错。

transcript.en: It's got a great atmosphere and before dinner you can go up on the roof and have a drink – it's really nice up there but you need to book.
transcript.cn: 那里氛围很棒，晚餐前你可以上屋顶喝一杯 —— 那里真的很棒，但你需要预订。

transcript.en: It's very popular as the views are spectacular.
transcript.cn: 它非常受欢迎，因为景色壮观。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Sounds good. So that's definitely a possibility then.
transcript.cn: 女人：听起来不错。那么，这确实是一种可能性。

transcript.en: Is there anywhere else you can think of?
transcript.cn: 你还能想到别的地方吗？

transcript.en: MAN: If you want somewhere a bit less formal then you could try Paloma.
transcript.cn: 男人：如果你想找个不那么正式的地方，可以试试帕洛玛。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Where's that? I haven't heard of it.
transcript.cn: 女人：在哪儿？我没听说过。

transcript.en: MAN: No, it's quite new. It's only been open a few months but it's got a great reputation already.
transcript.cn: 男人：不，那是一家很新的电影院。虽然才开业几个月，但已经享有盛誉了。

transcript.en: It's in a really beautiful old building on Bow Street.
transcript.cn: 它在弓街上一栋非常漂亮的老建筑里。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Oh, I think I know where you mean – right beside the cinema?
transcript.cn: 女人：哦，我想我知道你说的是哪里 —— 就在电影院旁边吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Yes, that's it. I've only been there a couple of times but I was really impressed.
transcript.cn: 男人：是的，就是这样。我只去过那里几次，但我真的印象深刻。

transcript.en: The chef used to work at Don Felipe's, apparently.
transcript.cn: 显然，那个厨师以前在费利佩先生那里工作过。

transcript.en: I was really sorry when that closed down.
transcript.cn: 那家店关门的时候我真的很难过。

transcript.en: WOMAN: So is all the food they serve Spanish, then?
transcript.cn: 女人：那么，他们提供的所有食物都是西班牙语的吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Yeah. You can get lots of small dishes to share – which always works really well if you're in a group.
transcript.cn: 男人：是啊。你可以分享很多小菜 —— 当你们在一起时，这种方式总是非常有效。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Mmm. Worth thinking about.
transcript.cn: 女人：嗯，值得考虑。

transcript.en: MAN: Yeah. There's a lively atmosphere and the waiters are really friendly.
transcript.cn: 男人：是的。那里气氛很活跃，服务员也非常友好。

transcript.en: The only thing is that you need to pay a £50 deposit to book a table.
transcript.cn: 唯一的问题是，你需要支付 50 英镑的订金才能预订一张桌子。

transcript.en: WOMAN: A lot of restaurants are doing that these days.
transcript.cn: 女人：现在很多餐厅都在这么做。

transcript.en: I should have a look at the menu to check there's a good choice of vegetarian dishes.
transcript.cn: 我应该看看菜单，看看有没有不错的素食菜肴。

transcript.en: A couple of my friends have stopped eating meat.
transcript.cn: 我有几个朋友已经不吃肉了。

transcript.en: MAN: Not sure. I'd say the selection of those would be quite limited.
transcript.cn: 男人：不确定。我觉得那些菜的选择会相当有限。

transcript.en: MAN: I've just thought of another idea.
transcript.cn: 男人：我刚刚想到了另一个主意。

transcript.en: Have you been to The Audley?
transcript.cn: 你去过奥德利吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN: No, don't think I've heard of it. How's it spelt?
transcript.cn: 女人：不，我想我没听说过。它是怎么拼写的？

transcript.en: MAN: A-U-D-L-E-Y. You must have heard of it – there's been a lot about it in the press.
transcript.cn: 男人：A-U-D-L-E-Y。你一定听说过 —— 报纸上有很多关于它的报道。

transcript.en: WOMAN: I don't tend to pay much attention to that kind of thing. So where is it exactly?
transcript.cn: 女人：我一般不太关注这种事。那么，它到底在哪里？

transcript.en: MAN: It's in that hotel near Baxter Bridge – on the top floor.
transcript.cn: 男人：在巴克斯特桥附近的那家酒店 —— 顶层。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Oh, the views would be incredible from up there.
transcript.cn: 女人：哦，从那里看到的景色一定很棒。

transcript.en: MAN: Yeah. I'd love to go.
transcript.cn: 男人：是的，我很想去。

transcript.en: I can't think of the chef's name but she was a judge on that TV cookery show recently.
transcript.cn: 我想不起那个厨师的名字了，不过她最近是那个电视烹饪节目的评委。

transcript.en: And she's written a couple of cookery books.
transcript.cn: 她还写了几本烹饪书。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Oh, Angela Frayn.
transcript.cn: 女人：哦，安吉拉・弗雷恩。

transcript.en: MAN: That's the one. Anyway, it's had excellent reviews from all the newspapers.
transcript.cn: 男人：就是那个。不管怎样，所有报纸都给了很好的评价。

transcript.en: WOMAN: That would be a memorable place for a celebration.
transcript.cn: 女人：那将是一个值得纪念的庆祝场所。

transcript.en: MAN: Definitely. Obviously it's worth going there just for the view but the food is supposed to be really special.
transcript.cn: 男人：确实如此。显然，仅仅为了欣赏风景而去那里是值得的，但食物应该非常特别。

transcript.en: WOMAN: She only likes cooking with local products, doesn't she?
transcript.cn: 女人：她只喜欢用本地产品做饭，对吧？

transcript.en: MAN: Yes. Everything at the restaurant has to be sourced within a short distance and absolutely nothing flown in from abroad.
transcript.cn: 男人：是的。餐厅里的所有食材都必须在短距离内采购，而且绝对不能有从国外空运进来的东西。

transcript.en: WOMAN: I imagine it's really expensive though.
transcript.cn: 女人：不过我想那一定很贵吧。

transcript.en: MAN: Well, you could go for the set lunch.
transcript.cn: 男人：好吧，你可以去吃套餐午餐。

transcript.en: That's quite reasonable for a top class restaurant: £30 a head.
transcript.cn: 对于一家顶级餐厅来说，这个价格相当合理：每人 30 英镑。

transcript.en: In the evening I think it'd be more like £50.
transcript.cn: 晚上的话，我觉得大概要 50 英镑。

transcript.en: WOMAN: At least that, I should think. But I'm sure everyone would enjoy it.
transcript.cn: 女人：至少我是这么认为的。但我相信每个人都会喜欢的。

transcript.en: It's not the kind of place you leave feeling hungry though, is it? With tiny portions?
transcript.cn: 不过，这不是那种会让你饿着肚子离开的地方，对吧？只吃很少的分量？

transcript.en: MAN: No, the reviews I've read didn't mention that. I imagine they'd be average.
transcript.cn: 男人：不，我读过的评论没有提到这一点。我想它们应该是一般的。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Well, that's all great, thanks …
transcript.cn: 女人：嗯，那太好了，谢谢......

groups.desc:
Questions 11-16
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: Heather says pottery differs from other art forms because

options.title: A
options.content: it lasts longer in the ground.

options.title: B
options.content: it is practised by more people.

options.title: C
options.content: it can be repaired more easily.

questions.analysis: 题目询问陶艺与其他艺术形式的不同之处。原文明确提到陶艺“stands the test of time”（经得起时间的考验），并对比指出“baskets and pictures don’t survive in the earth in the same way that pots do”（篮子和绘画无法像陶器那样在地下保存），这表明陶器在地下的保存时间更长，与选项A“it lasts longer in the ground”（它在地下保存更久）完全一致，因此A为正确答案。

questions.title: Archaeologists sometimes identify the use of ancient pottery from

options.title: A
options.content: the clay it was made with.

options.title: B
options.content: the marks that are on it.

options.title: C
options.content: the basic shape of it.

questions.analysis: 题目询问考古学家有时通过什么来确定古代陶器的用途。原文明确提到，考古学家通过“examining the impressions on the clay, the scratches from tools”（检查黏土上的印记、工具留下的划痕）来判断陶器是用于储存还是烹饪等用途。“impressions”（印记）和“scratches”（划痕）均属于“marks”（标记、痕迹）的范畴，因此选项B正确。

questions.title: Some people join Heather's pottery class because they want to

options.title: A
options.content: create an item that looks very old.

options.title: B
options.content: find something that they are good at.

options.title: C
options.content: make something that will outlive them.

questions.analysis: 题目询问“一些人参加Heather陶艺课的原因”。根据听力原文，当Heather提到“When I ask people why they want to take a pottery class with me”时，引出了“一些人”的动机。答案句中“they hope that something they create will also last longer than they do”和“many years after their death”明确表明，这些人希望自己创作的东西比自己存在得更久，即“make something that will outlive them”，与选项C表述一致，因此C为正确答案。

questions.title: What does Heather value most about being a potter?

options.title: A
options.content: its calming effect

options.title: B
options.content: its messy nature

options.title: C
options.content: its physical benefits

questions.analysis: Heather明确提到“what I love most”（对应题干“value most”）是制作陶器所需的专注，而这种专注“takes you away from the stresses of everyday life”（让你远离日常生活的压力），这与选项A的“calming effect”（平静效果）为同义转述，因此A是正确答案。

questions.title: Most of the visitors to Edelman Pottery

options.title: A
options.content: bring friends to join courses.

options.title: B
options.content: have never made a pot before.

options.title: C
options.content: try to learn techniques too quickly.

questions.analysis: 题目询问的是“Edelman Pottery的大多数访客”的情况。听力原文中，讲者提到“Like nearly everyone who comes here, I’m sure this is the first time you will have tried the art...”，其中“everyone who comes here”是题干中“visitors”的同义替换，而“the first time you will have tried the art”明确表明这些访客是第一次尝试陶艺，即“以前从未制作过陶器”，这与选项B的描述完全一致。

questions.title: Heather reminds her visitors that they should

options.title: A
options.content: put on their aprons.

options.title: B
options.content: change their clothes.

options.title: C
options.content: take off their jewellery.

questions.analysis: 题目问Heather提醒访客应该做什么。Heather在提到"Now, before we move on, can I just say a word about what you’re wearing."（在我们继续之前，我能说一下你们的穿着吗）之后，明确指出"please remove any watches, necklaces, etc."（请取下所有手表、项链等）。"watches, necklaces"（手表、项链）都属于"jewellery"（首饰）的范畴，"remove"（取下）与选项C中的"take off"（取下）是同义替换，所以答案是C。

collect_option.title: Which TWO things does Heather explain about kilns?

questions.analysis: 题目要求选出Heather对窑解释的两件事。定位词“kilns”在原文中以“kiln”为信号词出现。关于窑的功能，原文明确说明“去除黏土中的水分”并“让作品永久定型”，对应选项A；关于替代品，原文提到“烤箱”和“户外火坑”作为窑的替代物，对应选项E。因此正确答案为A和E。
题目要求选出Heather对窑解释的两件事。定位词“kilns”在原文中以“kiln”为信号词出现。关于窑的功能，原文明确说明“去除黏土中的水分”并“让作品永久定型”，对应选项A；关于替代品，原文提到“烤箱”和“户外火坑”作为窑的替代物，对应选项E。因此正确答案为A和E。

transcript.en: PART 2
Hello and welcome.
transcript.cn: 你好，欢迎光临。

transcript.en: My name's Heather McCallum and I'm one of the potters who work here at Edelman Pottery.
transcript.cn: 我叫希瑟・麦卡勒姆，是在埃德尔曼陶器厂工作的陶工之一。

transcript.en: Before we go into the workshop, I just want to say a bit about the craft of pottery.
transcript.cn: 在我们进入工作室之前，我想简单介绍一下陶器的制作工艺。

transcript.en: Then we'll have a look at the equipment, and you can try making a pot of your own.
transcript.cn: 然后我们会看看设备，你可以试着自己制作一个陶罐。

transcript.en: Like many people, I'm sure you know that pottery as an art form is tens of thousands of years old, and we know this because it stands the test of time.
transcript.cn: 和许多人一样，我相信你们都知道陶器作为一种艺术形式已经有数万年的历史，我们之所以知道这一点，是因为它经受住了时间的考验。

transcript.en: Things like baskets and pictures don't survive in the earth in the same way that pots do.
transcript.cn: 像篮子和画作这样的东西，不会像陶器那样在地球上保存下来。

transcript.en: And even if ancient pots are found in small pieces, they still provide a lot of information about the past.
transcript.cn: 即使古代陶器只是以小块碎片的形式被发现，它们仍然提供了大量关于过去的信息。

transcript.en: There's no doubt that pottery has given archaeologists a fascinating insight into how ancient humans lived.
transcript.cn: 毫无疑问，陶器为考古学家提供了关于古代人类生活方式的有趣见解。

transcript.en: The shape of an artefact may have been lost, but archaeologists can tell whether the pots were for, say, storage or cooking by examining the impressions on the clay, the scratches from tools; and the clay itself can reveal where the pots came from.
transcript.cn: 人工制品的形状可能已经遗失，但考古学家可以通过检查陶土上的痕迹、工具的刮痕来判断这些陶罐是用于储存还是烹饪；而陶土本身也能揭示这些陶罐的来源。

transcript.en: When I ask people why they want to take a pottery class with me, they sometimes talk about these things.
transcript.cn: 当我问人们为什么想和我一起上陶器课时，他们有时会谈论这些事情。

transcript.en: Like our ancestors, they hope that something they create will also last longer than they do, that their work – whether it is good or not – might say something about humanity many years after their death.
transcript.cn: 就像我们的祖先一样，他们希望自己创造的东西也能比他们存在的时间更长，希望他们的作品 —— 无论是好是坏 —— 能在他们去世多年后对人类有所启发。

transcript.en: Of course, you will all have your own reasons for coming here.
transcript.cn: 当然，你们来这里都有自己的理由。

transcript.en: As far as I'm concerned, what I love most is the concentration you need to make a good pot.
transcript.cn: 就我而言，我最喜欢的是制作好锅所需的专注力。

transcript.en: That focus takes you away from the stresses of everyday life.
transcript.cn: 这种专注能让你远离日常生活的压力。

transcript.en: If you're elderly, it's also good exercise for hands and wrists and helps with arthritis and, of course, it's a fun activity for children because it's so messy!
transcript.cn: 如果你是老年人，这也是一项有益于手和手腕的锻炼，有助于治疗关节炎。当然，对孩子们来说，这也是一项有趣的活动，因为它实在是太乱了！

transcript.en: Here at Edelman Pottery, we show you some of the basic pottery techniques so that you can use these to create whatever you wish – a gift for a friend, perhaps.
transcript.cn: 在埃德尔曼陶艺，我们向您展示一些基本的陶艺技巧，这样您就可以用它们创造任何您想要的东西 —— 也许是送给朋友的礼物。

transcript.en: Like nearly everyone who comes here, I'm sure this is the first time you will have tried the art, so we'll keep things simple today.
transcript.cn: 和几乎所有来到这里的人一样，我相信这是你第一次尝试这门艺术，所以今天我们会保持简单。

transcript.en: Now, before we move on, can I just say a word about what you're wearing.
transcript.cn: 现在，在我们继续之前，我能说一下你穿的是什么吗？

transcript.en: As we said in our email, please remove any watches, necklaces, etc. and put them somewhere safe.
transcript.cn: 正如我们在邮件中所说，请摘下任何手表、项链等物品，并将它们放在安全的地方。

transcript.en: If you have long hair, do tie it back now.
transcript.cn: 如果你有长发，现在就把它扎起来。

transcript.en: We'll provide aprons later, but I trust your clothes are old but comfortable – not your favourite T-shirt or jeans!
transcript.cn: 我们稍后会提供围裙，但我相信你的衣服虽然旧了，但很舒服 —— 不是你最喜欢的 T 恤或牛仔裤！

transcript.en: So now we're in the workshop. Have a look around – there's a lot going on.
transcript.cn: 现在我们来到了工作室。环顾四周 —— 发生了很多事情。

transcript.en: To make pottery that will last, you need a potter's wheel, a kiln – which is basically a very hot oven where you fire the pottery – and some tools.
transcript.cn: 要制作耐用的陶器，你需要一个陶轮、一个窑炉 —— 基本上就是一个非常热的烤箱，你在那里烧制陶器 —— 以及一些工具。

transcript.en: So first, the kiln. If you look over in the far corner, you'll see one of ours.
transcript.cn: 首先是窑。如果你看向远处的角落，你会看到我们的一个窑。

transcript.en: Since their invention, kilns have changed very little, though in the past 20 years, a lot of progress has been made in temperature control.
transcript.cn: 自发明以来，窑几乎没有什么变化，尽管在过去的 20 年里，温度控制方面取得了很大的进展。

transcript.en: Basically, a kiln removes the water from clay at temperatures of around a thousand degrees Celsius.
transcript.cn: 基本上，窑炉能在约 1000 摄氏度的温度下从粘土中提取水分。

transcript.en: This allows anything you've made to set permanently in shape.
transcript.cn: 这使得你制作的任何东西都能永久固定形状。

transcript.en: It's a pretty ugly, heavy object that's hard to keep in a house or flat so most people don't have one.
transcript.cn: 这是一个相当丑陋、沉重的物体，很难放在房子或公寓里，所以大多数人都没有炉子。

transcript.en: You may think, ‘Can't I use my oven?' Well, that's possible but domestic ovens don't really get hot enough and eventually the clay will crack and fall apart.
transcript.cn: 你可能会想：“我不能用我的炉子吗？” 好吧，这是可能的，但家用炉子并不能真正达到足够的热度，最终陶土会开裂和碎裂。

transcript.en: Some people fire pottery in a fire pit outside – but bear in mind that can be dangerous.
transcript.cn: 有些人会在外面的火坑里焚烧陶器 —— 但请记住，这可能是危险的。

transcript.en: You also need to know about safety procedures for kilns as they release toxic compounds into the air.
transcript.cn: 你还需要了解窑炉向空气中释放有毒化合物时的安全程序。

transcript.en: Every potter needs a potter's wheel.
transcript.cn: 每个陶工都需要一个陶轮。

transcript.en: This machine is used to shape the clay into an object with circular walls or sides – such as a bowl.
transcript.cn: 这种机器用于将粘土塑造成具有圆形边缘或侧面的物体，例如碗。

transcript.en: Its invention revolutionised the pottery industry, allowing multiple items to be produced in a day.
transcript.cn: 它的发明彻底改变了陶器行业，使得一天可以生产多种物品。

transcript.en: Lastly, there are a number of different tools that potters use, depending on what they want to make.
transcript.cn: 最后，陶工根据他们想要制作的物品，会使用多种不同的工具。

transcript.en: When you start, your hands can make all kinds of shapes and curves without relying on a sculpting tool.
transcript.cn: 当你开始时，你的双手可以在不依赖雕刻工具的情况下创造出各种形状和曲线。

transcript.en: However, there are some basic tools that you will need to handle the clay on the wheel.
transcript.cn: 然而，你需要一些基本的工具来处理车轮上的黏土。

transcript.en: Some look very strange and have even odder names that you may find hard to remember.
transcript.cn: 有些工具看起来非常奇怪，甚至有些奇怪的名字，你可能会发现很难记住。

transcript.en: Rather than go through them all now, I'll just name a few tools as we go along.
transcript.cn: 与其现在一一列举，我不如一边走一边说出几种工具的名字。

transcript.en: We can provide these and I wouldn't recommend spending money on them yet. So let's try making a pot of your own …
transcript.cn: 我们可以提供这些，我还不建议在它们上面花钱。所以让我们试着自己做一个锅吧......

questions.analysis: 学生们决定以自身经历作为演讲开头，Dev对此表示赞同并给出理由“Everyone will be able to relate to that”，其中“relate to”意为“理解并感同身受”，与选项B中的“recognise”（认识到，能理解）意思相近，且后文补充“a lot of students”进一步说明这是大多数学生能识别和理解的情况，因此答案选B。

questions.analysis: 题目问学生们同意与陌生人交谈是应对孤独的好策略的原因。听力中，塔玛拉提出“talking to strangers”后，德夫提到“interactions with shop assistants and bar staff make people feel more optimistic and relaxed”（对应选项C），但塔玛拉用“I don’t know about that but”转折，提出“it must make people feel more connected with their community”（与社区联系更紧密），德夫回应“True”表示同意。“connected with their community”（与社区联系紧密）与选项A的“creates a sense of belonging”（创造归属感）意思一致，因此A是正确答案。

questions.analysis: 题干询问学生难以理解为什么独处被认为是某种情况。原文中Tamara明确提到“It’s strange...”（这很奇怪，即难以理解），随后指出困惑的内容是“others love it”（另一些人喜欢独处）。“love it”（喜欢）直接对应“an enjoyable experience”（愉快的经历），因此正确答案为C。

transcript.en: PART 3
TAMARA: Shall we go through the notes we've made from our research into loneliness now, DEV?
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：德夫，我们现在来复习一下我们关于孤独的研究笔记好吗？

transcript.en: DEV: OK, Tamara. It's been a real eye-opener.
transcript.cn: 德夫：好的，塔玛拉。这真是让人大开眼界。

transcript.en: I had no idea that loneliness has been increasing steadily for the last twenty years.
transcript.cn: 我完全不知道过去二十年来，孤独感一直在稳步增长。

transcript.en: TAMARA: I know. And it's the same all over the world – the downside of a modern lifestyle I guess.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：我知道。全世界都是如此 —— 我猜这是现代生活方式的负面影响。

transcript.en: DEV: Did you come to any conclusions about what the reasons for the increase are?
transcript.cn: 德夫：关于孤独感增加的原因，你有什么结论吗？

transcript.en: TAMARA: Well, I'd assumed it was mainly an issue for the elderly – but in fact it's something which affects young people just as much.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：嗯，我原本以为这主要是老年人的问题 —— 但实际上，它对年轻人的影响同样严重。

transcript.en: DEV: Mmm. So nothing really to do with longer lifespans.
transcript.cn: 德夫：嗯。所以这与更长的寿命其实没什么关系。

transcript.en: What about social media?
transcript.cn: 那么社交媒体呢？

transcript.en: In my case, far from making me feel isolated, it actually does the opposite.
transcript.cn: 对我来说，孤独非但没有让我感到孤立，反而起到了相反的作用。

transcript.en: TAMARA: It definitely does more good than harm.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：这绝对是好处多于坏处。

transcript.en: I'd say loneliness has a lot to do with the way cities are designed – people living in high-rise flats with not much opportunity to speak to their neighbours.
transcript.cn: 我认为孤独感与城市的设计方式有很大关系 —— 人们住在高层公寓里，没有太多机会与邻居交谈。

transcript.en: DEV: I think you're right.
transcript.cn: 德夫：我觉得你说得对。

transcript.en: TAMARA: Another possible reason is that people are having fewer children and don't live in large extended family groups.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：另一个可能的原因是人们生育的孩子越来越少，而且不再生活在大型的大家庭群体中。

transcript.en: DEV: But, in this country anyway, that all changed decades ago and yet loneliness is a more recent problem.
transcript.cn: 德夫：但是，至少在这个国家，一切都在几十年前发生了变化，而孤独却是一个更近期的问题。

transcript.en: TAMARA: I suppose so. A more plausible explanation is that people are having to move around for work and often end up living miles away from their family and friends.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：我想是的。一个更合理的解释是，人们不得不四处奔波工作，最终常常住在离家人和朋友数英里之外的地方。

transcript.en: DEV: That's true.
transcript.cn: 德夫：确实如此。

transcript.en: TAMARA: Looking at the studies on health risks and loneliness, there are claims that loneliness has as much impact as smoking 15 cigarettes a day.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：看看关于健康风险和孤独感的研究，有人声称孤独感的影响相当于每天吸 15 支烟。

transcript.en: DEV: Or similar to the risks caused by obesity. But I'm not sure there's enough evidence for some of these claims.
transcript.cn: 德夫：或者类似于肥胖引起的风险。但我不确定是否有足够的证据支持这些说法。

transcript.en: TAMARA: Well – what about that one in Finland which showed that loneliness increased the risk of cancer by about ten per cent? And those findings have been supported by other studies too.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：那么，芬兰的那项研究呢？那项研究表明，孤独会使癌症风险增加约 10%。这些发现也得到了其他研究的支持。

transcript.en: DEV: You're right about that one. I was actually thinking of the studies on dementia.
transcript.cn: 德夫：你说得对。我其实是在想关于痴呆症的研究。

transcript.en: Some found no association between loneliness and dementia and others found the opposite.
transcript.cn: 一些人发现孤独与痴呆症之间没有关联，而另一些人则发现了相反的结果。

transcript.en: TAMARA: Not exactly reliable then.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：那就不太可靠了。

transcript.en: There's been a lot of research on cardiovascular disease and whether loneliness contributes to that.
transcript.cn: 关于心血管疾病以及孤独是否会导致心血管疾病的研究很多。

transcript.en: DEV: Yes, I read that it was hard to reach a judgement as the definition of loneliness varied quite a lot and the responses from participants were too subjective.
transcript.cn: 德夫：是的，我读到过，由于孤独的定义差异很大，而且参与者的反应过于主观，因此很难做出判断。

transcript.en: But there's no doubt that loneliness contributes to a weakened immune system.
transcript.cn: 但毫无疑问，孤独会导致免疫系统减弱。

transcript.en: TAMARA: Unquestionably. The data on that is sound.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：毫无疑问。关于这一点的数据是可靠的。

transcript.en: DEV: What did you think about the evolutionary theory of loneliness?
transcript.cn: 德夫：你对孤独的进化理论有什么看法？

transcript.en: TAMARA: Well, I thought the idea that loneliness evolved because it motivated people to be with other people quite convincing.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：嗯，我认为孤独感之所以能够进化，是因为它能够以令人信服的方式激励人们与他人在一起。

transcript.en: Survival often depended on group cooperation.
transcript.cn: 生存往往依赖于群体合作。

transcript.en: DEV: But I don't think there's enough evidence to claim that there must be a group of neurons in our brains which influence social behaviour, by making us feel bad when we're alone.
transcript.cn: 德夫：但我认为没有足够的证据证明，我们大脑中一定存在一组神经元，它们通过让我们独处时感到不舒服来影响社交行为。

transcript.en: TAMARA: There are a few studies which support the theory but not conclusively enough. More evidence is needed.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：有一些研究支持这个理论，但还不够确凿。需要更多的证据。

transcript.en: DEV: Mmm. And anyway, this theory's not really useful when it comes to solving the problem of loneliness today.
transcript.cn: 德夫：嗯。无论如何，这个理论在解决当今的孤独问题时并没有真正的用处。

transcript.en: TAMARA: True.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：没错。

transcript.en: DEV: Should we look at the relationship between loneliness and mental health now?
transcript.cn: 德夫：我们现在应该研究孤独感与心理健康之间的关系吗？

transcript.en: TAMARA: OK. So, loneliness and depression are clearly related and that's been recognised by various governments around the world.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：好的。那么，孤独感和抑郁症显然是相关的，这一点已经得到了世界各国政府的认可。

transcript.en: But unlike depression, loneliness has no recognised clinical form.
transcript.cn: 但与抑郁症不同的是，孤独症没有公认的临床表现形式。

transcript.en: DEV: There's no available diagnosis or effective treatment – and that's not likely to change.
transcript.cn: 德夫：目前还没有可用的诊断或有效的治疗方法 —— 这种情况不太可能改变。

transcript.en: TAMARA: I don't think so either. I was thinking we should start our presentation with an example from our own experience.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：我也不这么认为。我在想，我们应该从我们自己的经历中举个例子来开始我们的演讲。

transcript.en: I'd like to talk about how lonely I was when I started university.
transcript.cn: 我想谈谈我开始上大学时有多孤独。

transcript.en: Being away from home for the first time and all that.
transcript.cn: 第一次离开家，诸如此类。

transcript.en: DEV: Good idea. Everyone will be able to relate to that – although a lot of students were probably too embarrassed to admit to it.
transcript.cn: 德夫：好主意。每个人都能与之产生共鸣 —— 尽管许多学生可能因为太害羞而不敢承认。

transcript.en: TAMARA: Yeah. We could discuss ways of dealing with loneliness as well. Like just talking to strangers.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：是啊。我们也可以讨论一下如何应对孤独。比如只是和陌生人聊天。

transcript.en: DEV: Loads of studies have shown that interactions with shop assistants and bar staff make people feel more optimistic and relaxed.DEV:
transcript.cn: 大量研究表明，与店员和酒吧工作人员互动会让人感到更加乐观和放松。

transcript.en: TAMARA: I don't know about that but it must make people feel more connected with their community.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：我不知道这个，但它肯定能让人们感到与社区更加亲近。

transcript.en: DEV: True. Although you need to be a certain kind of person to be able to just strike up a conversation.
transcript.cn: 德夫：没错。不过，你需要成为某种特定类型的人，才能轻松地开启对话。

transcript.en: TAMARA: Good point. We should say something about solitude and how being alone and being lonely aren't the same thing.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：说得好。我们应该谈谈孤独，以及为什么独处和孤独不是一回事。

transcript.en: It's strange the way some people can't stand being by themselves while others love it.
transcript.cn: 奇怪的是，有些人无法忍受独处，而另一些人却乐在其中。

transcript.en: DEV: Yeah. The research shows a certain amount of solitude is beneficial for well-being, which I appreciate – but being alone isn't something I actually like.
transcript.cn: 德夫：是的。研究表明，一定程度的独处对身心健康有益，我很欣赏这一点 —— 但独处并不是我真正喜欢的事情。

transcript.en: I'd never choose to go on holiday alone, for example.
transcript.cn: 例如，我绝不会选择独自度假。

transcript.en: TAMARA: Me neither.
transcript.cn: 塔玛拉：我也是。

transcript.en: DEV: Well let's not …
transcript.cn: 德夫：好吧，我们别......

groups.title: Reclaiming urban rivers
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Historical background · Nearly all major cities were built on a river. · Rivers were traditionally used by city dwellers for transport, fishing and recreation. · Industrial development and rising populations later led to: - more sewage from houses being discharged into the river - pollution from $21985$ on the river bank. · In 1957, the River Thames in London was declared biologically $21986$. Recent improvements · Seals and even a $21987$ have been seen in the River Thames. · Riverside warehouses are converted to restaurants and $21988$. · In Los Angeles, there are plans to: - build a riverside $21989$. - display 36 $21990$ projects. · In Paris, $21991$ are created on the sides of the river every summer. Transport possibilities · Over 2 billion passengers already travel by $21992$ in cities round the world. · Changes in shopping habits mean the number of deliveries that are made is increasing. · Instead of road transport, goods could be transported by large freight barges and electric $21993$ or, in future, by $21994$.

questions.analysis: 同学们，我们来看这道题。题目问的是“来自河岸上【什么】的污染”。首先，我们用题干中的“pollution”和“river bank”作为定位词去听力原文中找。在听力里，我们听到了“other types of pollution increased”，这里的“pollution”和题干完全一样。然后，对于“river bank”，原文用了“beside the river”来表达，这就是它的同义替换。找到对应的句子后，我们看到“as factories were built beside the river”，这句话明确告诉我们，是“factories”（工厂）被建在了河边，并且它们排放废料造成了污染。所以，“pollution from”后面接的来源就是“factories”。之前提到的“sewage”（污水）是另一种污染源，而“waste materials”是工厂排放的污染物，不是污染的来源。因此，这道题的正确答案就是“factories”。

这道题的答案是“dead”。首先，我们通过题干中的定位词“1957”和“River Thames”能快速在听力原文中找到对应的句子。原文明确提到“1957年，科学家宣布the River Thames was ‘dead’ in biological terms”，这里“in biological terms”（在生物学意义上）和题干中的“biologically”（生物学上地）是同义转换，而“declared that...was”与题干的“was declared”结构一致，所以“dead”就是被宣布的内容，因此答案是“dead”。

这道题的答案是“whale”。首先，我们通过题干中的定位词“Seals”和“River Thames”在听力原文中定位到关键信息。当听到“River Thames”时，说明进入了相关段落；接着听到“seals swimming in the water”，与题干中的“Seals... have been seen”对应。题干结构是“Seals and even a...”，表明需要填一个与“Seals”并列的事物（即另一种动物）。原文中“and”连接的后半句提到“rescue a whale”，这里的“whale”就是和“seals”并列的、在泰晤士河被发现的动物，且题目要求“ONE WORD ONLY”，所以“whale”是正确答案。

同学你看，这道题问的是“Riverside warehouses are converted to restaurants and 【 】.”。首先，我们在题干中找到定位词“Riverside warehouses”、“converted”和“restaurants”。在听力原文中，我们听到了“Warehouses... are now being converted into expensive restaurants and also into apartments...”。这里，“Warehouses”对应题干的“Riverside warehouses”，“converted”原词复现，“restaurants”也原词出现。题目中“and”表示并列关系，说明空格处应填与“restaurants”并列的、仓库被改造成的另一种事物。原文中“restaurants”后面紧跟着“and also into apartments”，所以“apartments”就是我们要找的答案。而且题目要求“ONE WORD ONLY”，“apartments”是一个单词，符合要求。

题目问的是“build a riverside 【 】”，即“建造一个河边的什么”。我们首先看定位词“build”，在原文中它的同义替换词是“make”；另一个定位词“riverside”，在原文中对应的同义替换是“the banks of the river”。当听到“Los Angeles”这个地名信号时，紧接着出现了“the banks of the river”和“make”，而“make”后面接的名词就是“park”，所以“make a park”就是“build a riverside park”的意思，因此答案是“park”。

这道题需要你填“展示什么类型的项目”。首先，通过题干中的定位词“display”和“projects”，你可以在听力中快速找到关键信息。录音中提到“displaying projects”，这里“displaying”是“display”的动词形式变化，属于同义替换，而“projects”直接复现，说明答案就在附近。接着听，后面说“related to various kinds of art”（与各种艺术相关的），这部分是修饰“projects”的，明确指出了项目的类型是“艺术相关的”。题目要求填一个单词，所以核心词“art”就是答案。注意不要错填“local”（修饰“people”，指当地人，不是项目类型）或“various”（修饰“kinds”，指种类，不是项目核心类型），只有“art”准确对应“展示什么项目”的问题。

同学你看，这道题问的是在巴黎，每年夏天河岸边会被建成什么。首先，我们用题干中的“Paris”作为定位词，在听力中很容易就能听到“In the city of Paris”，这就帮我们锁定了答案所在的大致位置。接着，题干中的“every summer”在原文中被同义替换成了“during the summer months of July and August”，听到这里就要特别注意后面的内容了。原文明确说“the banks are transformed into beaches”，“transformed into”的意思就是“被改造成、被建成”，这和题干里的“are created”表达的是同一个意思。所以，河岸被改造成的就是“beaches”，这个词直接对应了题目的空格，因此答案就是“beaches”。

同学你看，这道题我们首先在题干中找到定位词“Over 2 billion passengers”。在听力原文中，我们听到了它的同义替换“more than two billion passengers”，这就帮我们锁定了答案句。答案句明确说“use the ferry to travel”，而题干问的是“travel by 【 】”，“by ferry”就是“use the ferry to travel”的常用表达方式，所以这里的答案自然就是“ferry”啦，而且它也符合题目要求的“ONE WORD ONLY”。

这道题的答案是“bikes”。首先，我们通过题干中的定位词“electric”在听力原文中寻找对应信息。原文提到“electric ones”，这里的“ones”是指代前文的“cargo bikes”（货运自行车）。题目要求填写一个单词，“cargo bikes”中“cargo”是修饰词，核心名词是“bikes”，所以“electric ones”实际指的就是“electric bikes”。因此，空格处应填入“bikes”。

这道题的答案是“drone”。首先，我们通过题干中的定位词“future”来锁定答案位置。在听力原文中，与“future”对应的信号词是“in future”，当听到“And in future...”时，就说明答案即将出现。接着，答案句明确提到“the final stage could even be carried out by drone”，这里的“carried out by”与题干中的“transported by”意思一致，都是“由……运输/执行”的含义。题干要求填入“in future, by”后面的运输方式，而“drone”是这句话中唯一表示运输工具的单数名词，完全符合题目“ONE WORD ONLY”的要求，所以答案是“drone”。

transcript.en: PART 4
It's quite hard to think of a city that doesn't have a big river running through it.
transcript.cn: 很难想象一个没有大河流经的城市。

transcript.en: If you think about the major cities in the world, Shanghai, New York, Mumbai, London, they're nearly all built on rivers.
transcript.cn: 如果你想想世界上的主要城市，如上海、纽约、孟买、伦敦，它们几乎都建立在河流之上。

transcript.en: When these cities were established hundreds or even thousands of years ago, the rivers were a big part of people's lives.
transcript.cn: 当这些城市在数百甚至数千年前建立时，河流是人们生活的重要组成部分。

transcript.en: In 16th-century London, the quickest way to get from one part of the city to another was by river.
transcript.cn: 在 16 世纪的伦敦，从城市的一个部分到另一个部分最快的方式是通过河流。

transcript.en: But people also used the river for fishing, as the water then was relatively clean, and they would also go on boat trips up and down the river just for pleasure, as a relaxing escape from the noise and bustle of the city streets.
transcript.cn: 但人们也会在这条河上钓鱼，因为当时的河水相对清澈。他们还会乘船在河上来回游玩，仅仅是为了娱乐，作为一种放松的方式，逃离城市街道的喧嚣和繁忙。

transcript.en: But as industries developed and populations increased, city rivers suffered.
transcript.cn: 然而，随着工业的发展和人口的增长，城市河流受到了影响。

transcript.en: The rising number of people meant there was a huge increase in the amount of sewage discharged into the rivers.
transcript.cn: 人口数量的增加意味着向河流排放的污水量大幅增加。

transcript.en: Rivers had always been used for this purpose, but when the number of inhabitants was so small that wasn't such a problem.
transcript.cn: 河流一直被用于这个目的，但当居民数量如此之少时，这就不是什么大问题了。

transcript.en: However, as cities grew to over a million inhabitants, the impact on the rivers became more serious.
transcript.cn: 然而，随着城市人口增长到超过一百万，对河流的影响变得更加严重。

transcript.en: In addition, other types of pollution increased as factories were built beside the river and discharged their waste materials into the water.
transcript.cn: 此外，随着工厂在河边建立并将废弃物排放到水中，其他类型的污染也在增加。

transcript.en: This got worse over time.
transcript.cn: 随着时间的推移，情况变得越来越糟糕。

transcript.en: As recently as 1957, scientists at London's Natural History Museum declared that the River Thames was ‘dead' in biological terms, as the water was too filthy to support any kind of life.
transcript.cn: 直到 1957 年，伦敦自然历史博物馆的科学家们才宣布，从生物学角度来看，泰晤士河已经 “死亡”, 因为河水太过污秽，无法支持任何形式的生命。

transcript.en: But in recent years, as rivers lost their industrial function, cities have begun to recognise their true value and to take steps to clean them up.
transcript.cn: 但近年来，随着泰晤士河失去其工业功能，城市开始认识到它们的真正价值，并采取措施来清理它们。

transcript.en: For example, the River Thames is now cleaner than it's been for a hundred and fifty years.
transcript.cn: 例如，泰晤士河现在比过去一百五十年都要干净。

transcript.en: These days you can see seals swimming in the water and recently people had to try to rescue a whale which had got lost and swum up the river from the sea by mistake.
transcript.cn: 如今，你可以看到海豹在水中游动。最近，人们不得不试图拯救一头迷路的鲸鱼，它误从海中游上了泰晤士河。

transcript.en: Unfortunately they didn't succeed, but the problem was disorientation rather than the quality of the water.
transcript.cn: 不幸的是，他们没有成功，但问题不在于水质，而在于方向感。

transcript.en: Then, all around the world, riverside areas are now seen as prime sites for development.
transcript.cn: 然后，在全世界范围内，河流沿岸地区现在被视为主要的开发地点。

transcript.en: Warehouses that were once used for storing goods are now being converted into expensive restaurants and also into apartments with river views, which are in great demand and sell for astronomical prices.
transcript.cn: 曾经用于储存货物的仓库如今正被改造成昂贵的餐厅，同时也被改造成了能够欣赏河景的公寓。这些公寓非常受欢迎，售价高得惊人。

transcript.en: In Los Angeles, on the west coast of the USA, an architect has plans to revitalise the banks of the river and to make a park there which can provide facilities for sports as well as a natural environment for relaxing in.
transcript.cn: 在美国西海岸的洛杉矶，一位建筑师计划重振河岸，并在那里建造一个公园，为运动提供设施，同时营造一个适合放松的自然环境。

transcript.en: It's also hoped that the riverside can be used for other purposes – it's been proposed that facilities could be provided for displaying projects related to various kinds of art that have been produced by local people, for example.
transcript.cn: 还希望河岸能够用于其他目的 —— 例如，有人提议可以提供设施来展示当地人创作的各种艺术作品。

transcript.en: In the city of Paris, during the summer months of July and August, all the traffic is banned from the roads by the sides of the river and the banks are transformed into beaches, where people can relax in deckchairs under potted palm trees, sunbathe, or buy a drink or a snack while enjoying the view.
transcript.cn: 在巴黎市，在夏季的 7 月和 8 月，所有交通都被禁止进入河岸两侧的道路，河岸被改造成海滩，人们可以在盆栽棕榈树下的帆布躺椅上放松身心，享受日光浴，或者一边欣赏风景一边购买饮料或零食。

transcript.en: But to make the most of our rivers in our increasingly crowded cities, we need to allow them to regain their original purpose and be used as a means of transport, reclaiming our streets from cars and lorries.
transcript.cn: 但是，为了在我们日益拥挤的城市中充分利用我们的河流，我们需要允许它们恢复其最初的用途，被用作交通工具，从汽车和卡车手中夺回我们的街道。

transcript.en: To do this we'll have to shift more traffic back to the river, but this time cleanly and silently, making the most of modern technology.
transcript.cn: 要做到这一点，我们必须将更多的交通流量转移到河边，但这次要干净利落、悄无声息，充分利用现代技术。

transcript.en: Already more than two billion passengers use the ferry to travel in cities around the world like Istanbul, San Francisco and New York.
transcript.cn: 已经有超过 20 亿乘客在世界各地的城市如伊斯坦布尔、旧金山和纽约乘坐渡轮旅行。

transcript.en: And these numbers are set to rise further.
transcript.cn: 而且这些数字还将进一步上升。

transcript.en: Admittedly, it's not a fast way of travelling, but neither is a car when it's stuck in traffic.
transcript.cn: 诚然，这不是一种快速的旅行方式，但当汽车陷入交通堵塞时，它也同样不是。

transcript.en: Of course, passenger traffic on roads might decrease as more people start working from home.
transcript.cn: 当然，随着越来越多的人开始在家工作，道路上的客流量可能会减少。

transcript.en: But another recent development, the huge rise in online shopping, has meant that another form of urban traffic just keeps on growing, and that's deliveries.
transcript.cn: 但最近的另一个发展，即网上购物的巨大增长，意味着城市交通的另一种形式仍在持续增长，那就是配送。

transcript.en: Trucks and vans in the city pollute and double park while dropping off parcels.
transcript.cn: 城市中的卡车和货车在卸下包裹时会污染环境，并且会停两次车。

transcript.en: Imagine using the immense capacity of shipping to take these trucks off the road.
transcript.cn: 想象一下，利用巨大的运输能力让这些卡车偏离道路。

transcript.en: One freight barge can replace 44 large trucks, uses far less energy and causes less pollution.
transcript.cn: 一艘货运驳船可以替换 44 辆大型卡车，消耗的能源少得多，造成的污染也少得多。

transcript.en: When the barge docks at the riverside, the parcels could be taken the last few kilometres to their final destination on cargo bikes – electric ones, of course.
transcript.cn: 当驳船停靠在河边时，包裹可以通过货运自行车 (当然是电动自行车) 运送到最终目的地，完成最后几公里的运输。

transcript.en: This is already happening in the Dutch city of Amsterdam.
transcript.cn: 这种情况已经在荷兰城市阿姆斯特丹发生。

transcript.en: And in future, the final stage could even be carried out by drone, although at present this isn't allowed.
transcript.cn: 在未来，最后阶段甚至可以由无人机执行，尽管目前这是不允许的。

transcript.en: Wouldn't it be great to unblock our city centres in this way! Looking further ahead ...
transcript.cn: 如果能用这种方式解锁我们的城市中心，岂不是太棒了！展望未来......
